tracked_cves.summary: A potential memory leak issue was discovered in SDL2 in GLES_CreateTexture() in SDL_render_gles.c, allowing denial of service.
tracked_cves.included_because: This is a resource-lifecycle bug, not a memory-corruption bug.
rust_port_focus: Use RAII for renderer and texture creation so partially initialized resources are always cleaned up.
Add failure-path tests for GLES texture creation and other backend allocation paths.

tracked_cves.summary: SDL_BlitCopy in video/SDL_blit_copy.c had an integer overflow, with heap corruption as the C-language consequence, when processing crafted BMP input.
tracked_cves.included_because: The root issue is unchecked size arithmetic, even though the published exploit path ends in memory corruption.
rust_port_focus: Centralize checked_mul and checked_add handling for surface sizes, pitches, and blit copy lengths.
Fuzz malformed BMP headers that force extreme dimensions or row sizes.

tracked_cves.summary: Fill_IMA_ADPCM_block in audio/SDL_wave.c had a heap over-read caused by an integer overflow in IMA_ADPCM_decode().
tracked_cves.included_because: The summary identifies integer overflow as the root cause, so the bug class is parser arithmetic validation rather than purely memory corruption.
rust_port_focus: Use checked arithmetic for ADPCM block-size and sample-count derivation.
Reject malformed WAVE metadata before allocating or stepping decode state.

tracked_cves.summary: Creating a new RGB surface in SDL 2.0.5 could integer-overflow, under-allocate, and then lead to a later buffer overflow on crafted image input.
tracked_cves.included_because: The core defect is unchecked dimension and allocation math.
rust_port_focus: Provide one checked surface-size calculation path shared by all surface constructors.
Reject dimensions whose total byte size cannot fit in the chosen allocation and indexing model.
